text:
Market today: Mixed signals for tech. While PayPal M&A suggests value, Cramer's call for "cold hard proof" on AI could temper the sector's biggest driver. Overall, no strong market-wide tailwinds for your concentrated tech/chip book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-34.95%) and SPAL (-37.57%) are your main concerns. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning drawdowns hurt extra on margin, and they decay over time.

Watch: Keep a close eye on any shift in AI sentiment and general market volatility, especially its impact on your leveraged positions.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged losing positions; don't chase new buys without strong conviction.
plays: Respect SOXL's stop at 200; consider trimming further given its -34.95% loss and leveraged nature.
Monitor SPAL closely with its -37.57% loss; ensure its stop at 21.38 is firm due to its leveraged, fast-moving nature.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The headlines don't provide a compelling, specific catalyst for new tech/chip buys that also align with scanner setups.
